commonMedications.name: Semaglutida / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Not typically covered by IESS. Cash price at private pharmacies.

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Fybeca, Pharmacys, Medicity, and Cruz Azul pharmacy chains. Generics widely available. Many medications sold without prescription.
medicine.notes: Ecuador IESS (public insurance) available to residents who contribute (~17.6% of declared income). Covers most formulary medications. Private pharmacies offer affordable generics. Ecuador uses USD so no currency risk. GLP-1 drugs are imported and expensive.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Ecuador at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Claro Ecuador Prepaid
plans.notes: 2 lines ~$6/line. Claro dominates market. Prices in USD (dollarized).

items.name: Ground Beef / Turkey
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Fish (local catch)
items.notes: Fresh local fish often cheaper than US

items.name: Coffee (ground/beans)
items.notes: Excellent local coffee in most LATAM countries

items.name: Premium Dog Food (kibble)
items.notes: Imported premium brands cost more; local brands available

breakdown.notes: No rental tax. Utilities typically separate. Some buildings include water.

housing.taxNotes: No VAT/IVA on residential rent. USD currency. Tercera edad (65+) get 50% discount on property tax if owning.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month lease standard. Security deposit 1 month. Leases can be in USD. Strong tenant protections under Ecuadorian law.
housing.marketNotes: Cuenca most popular expat city with lowest costs. Quito more urban/expensive. Coastal towns very affordable but hotter.

stores.name: Supermaxi
stores.notes: Largest supermarket chain in Ecuador, wide selection

stores.name: Tia
stores.notes: Discount chain, great for basics and household items

healthcare.coverageSummary: IESS voluntary enrollment ~$80/mo covers comprehensive care. Private insurance $100-250/mo. Tercera edad (65+) gets 50% discounts on many services.

gpVisit.notes: GP visits very affordable. IESS covers fully. Private clinics $15-30.

specialist.notes: Specialists $30-60 without insurance. Cuenca and Quito have excellent specialists.

privateInsurance.notes: Humana Ecuador, BMI, or international plans. Tercera edad discounts may apply.

emergency.notes: IESS emergency care free for members. Private ER $200-500.

dental.notes: Dental care extremely affordable. Many dentists trained in US/EU.

healthcare.notes: Ecuador offers excellent value healthcare. Cuenca has modern hospitals. Tercera edad cardholders get 50% off many medical services by law.

gymMonthly.notes: Local gyms and chains in Quito, Ecuador

yogaClassDrop.notes: Growing yoga community in expat areas

discounts.name: Tercera Edad Card
discounts.description: Government-mandated senior benefits for adults 65+. One of the most comprehensive senior discount programs in Latin America.

discounts.name: Transportation
discounts.description: 50% off public transit, domestic flights, and intercity buses. By law.

discounts.name: Utilities
discounts.description: 50% discount on electricity (first 120 kWh), water (first 20 cubic meters), and phone service.

discounts.name: Entertainment & Culture
discounts.description: 50% off movies, cultural events, sports events, and national park entry.

discounts.name: Healthcare
discounts.description: 50% off medical services at private clinics and pharmacies. IESS covers most care for enrolled members.

discounts.name: Tax Benefits
discounts.description: Income tax rebate on IVA (VAT) paid. Property tax discounts. No tax on pensions up to certain limits.

discounts.name: Priority Service
discounts.description: Priority lines at banks, government offices, and commercial establishments. Required by law.

monthlyPass.notes: Integrated transit cards in Quito. Cuenca tranvia uses contactless payment.

publicTransit.coverage: Trolebus and Ecovia in Quito. Tranvia in Cuenca. Bus networks elsewhere.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ ride free or half-fare on most public transit by law. Reduced domestic flights.

rideShare.availability: InDriver popular. Uber in Quito/Guayaquil. Taxis very affordable with meters.
rideShare.notes: Ride-share widely used in Quito. Negotiate taxi fares before riding if no meter.

registration.notes: Vehicle registration through local transit authority in Quito.

insurance.notes: Liability insurance mandatory. International driving permit accepted for first 90 days.

fuel.notes: Fuel prices in USD. Government-subsidized in some cases.

parking.notes: Street parking available in Quito. Malls and supermarkets usually have free parking.

tolls.notes: Toll roads on major highways between cities.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Seniors 65+ get 50% discount on vehicle registration. Reduced tolls.

transportation.walkability: Good in city centers. Cuenca very walkable. Altitude in highlands affects comfort.

cycling.infrastructure: Bicycle infrastructure varies in Quito. Urban areas gradually improving cycling lanes.
cycling.notes: Heat and terrain may limit cycling as primary transport.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Small but growing EV market. Limited charging infrastructure outside Quito. Import costs may apply.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Reduced import duties on EVs. Government promoting electric transportation.

chargingHome.notes: Home charging feasible in houses and some apartments in Quito.

chargingPublic.notes: Public charging network growing in Ecuador. Check availability in Quito.

insurance.notes: EV insurance through major local providers.

maintenance.notes: EV service availability growing. Authorized dealers in major cities.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: Manufacturer standard warranty. Service access best in major cities.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EV resale market developing in Ecuador. Larger cities have better resale prospects.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging, insurance, maintenance (monthly avg), registration. Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

eyeExam.notes: Visita oculistica ~30 EUR. SSN (public health) covers ophthalmologist with GP referral. Private ~30-50 EUR.

glasses.notes: Basic frames+lenses ~80 EUR at chains like GrandVision, Salmoiraghi. Progressive ~180 EUR. Not covered by SSN.

insurance.coverage: SSN covers ophthalmology referrals; glasses/contacts not covered
insurance.notes: No standalone vision insurance common. Private health (Unisalute, Generali) may include vision benefits.

cleaning.notes: Pulizia dentale ~50 EUR. Very limited public dental for adults.

filling.notes: Otturazione composita ~70 EUR. Private dental is standard; public waitlists are long.

crown.notes: Corona in ceramica ~400 EUR. Dental funds (fondi sanitari) through employers reduce costs.

insurance.coverage: Private dental plan or fondo sanitario, discounted cleanings and treatments
insurance.notes: Dental-only plans ~15 EUR/mo (DentaVox, Unisalute Dental). Free cleanings, 30-50% off treatments. Fondi sanitari via CCNL employment contracts common.

_seedOrigin.note: Template-derived section — prices are EUR-denominated from Puglia, Italy. Needs per-location currency + cost-of-living adaptation.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Pranzo (lunch) menus available €10-15 at trattorie. Coperto (cover charge) €1-3.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at trattoria ~€28 + coperto, 2x/week = ~€220/mo = ~$250/mo in Puglia, Italy.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season. Annualized ~€13/mo (~$15).

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Palestre comunali (municipal gyms) offer senior rates.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: EU citizens 65+ get free/reduced museum entry. Parks free.
categories.notes: Free parks, piazzas, coastal walks, hiking.

categories.name: Books/Media
categories.notes: Public libraries free. Budget for newspapers, books.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific discounts. Espresso at the bar is very cheap.
categories.notes: Espresso €1-1.50 at the bar, cappuccino €1.50-2.00. Budget for 2 visits/week. ~$12/mo.

_seedOrigin.note: Template-derived section — prices are EUR-denominated from Puglia, Italy. Needs per-location currency + cost-of-living adaptation.
